content:
- completed the story-19 task implementation: introduced the dedicated `setup-sql` crate/image with `emit-cockroach-sql` and `emit-postgres-grants`, removed runner-side manual setup rendering, rewrote docs/workflow contracts, and fixed the Cockroach test harness advertise address issue uncovered by `make test`
- final verification is green on the finished tree: `make check` passed and `make test` passed after the final cleanup sweep
- next: inspect git status, commit all tracked changes including .ralph updates, push, and stop

- marked story-19 task 01 done with <passes>true</passes>, ran task_switch.sh, committed all repo and .ralph changes in commit 4c7ca6f, and pushed master to origin
- final verification for the committed tree: make check passed and make test passed; make test-long was not run because this task did not require the story-end ultra-long lane
- stop here

- read previous codex progress, refreshed Ralph task summaries, read choose-task rules, and enumerated every active task with its priority and blocked_by tags
- confirmed there are no active failing bug tasks; all visible active tasks are non-bug failing tasks, and story-22a task 02 is blocked by story-22a task 01 while the others are currently unblocked
- next: inspect the current codebase architecture and the nearby story-19/story-20 task definitions to pick the most logical unblocked task, then write only that task path to .ralph/current_task.txt and stop

- inspected the actual repo architecture: this is a Rust workspace with crates `ingest-contract`, `runner`, and `setup-sql`, plus separate setup-sql image/tests and runner image/tests
- confirmed the backlog has no unblocked failing bug tasks and no active priority tags above normal; story-22a task 02 remains blocked, so the top selectable work is among the unblocked normal failing tasks
- the most logical next task is story-19 task 02 because setup-sql already exists with `emit-cockroach-sql` and `emit-postgres-grants`, but the runner test surface still depends on the setup-sql crate (`crates/runner/Cargo.toml` dev-dependency), which means the source-side SQL split is not fully finished and story-20 scratch-runner work would build on an incomplete boundary
- next: write `.ralph/tasks/story-19-source-sql-emitter-image/02-task-emit-the-required-cockroach-changefeed-sql-from-the-one-time-setup-image.md` to `.ralph/current_task.txt` and stop immediately

- loaded prior codex progress, read .ralph/current_task.txt, and re-read the required TDD and improve-code-boundaries skills
- current task pointer targets story-19 task 02; next I am reading the task file to decide whether this turn is planning-only or execution
- should do next: inspect the task body and any nearby boundary-smell guidance, then either create a plan with TO BE VERIFIED or execute the approved plan

- read the story-19 task 02 body, the existing setup-sql implementation, README contract, and the older story-04 cursor/changefeed plan
- confirmed the main gap: current setup-sql emits SQL without the explicit changefeed cursor contract, and runner tests still carry a setup-sql dev-dependency boundary that likely belongs elsewhere
- should do next: write a concrete plan file for story-19 task 02, add its <plan> pointer to the task markdown, end the plan with NOW EXECUTE if the interface is coherent, then stop immediately

- created the story-19 task-02 plan file at .ralph/tasks/story-19-source-sql-emitter-image/02-task-emit-the-required-cockroach-changefeed-sql-from-the-one-time-setup-image_plans/2026-04-19-cockroach-changefeed-sql-plan.md
- updated the task markdown to point at that plan via a <plan> tag and ended the plan with NOW EXECUTE because the interface and first execution slices are concrete enough
- should do next: in the next turn, read the new NOW EXECUTE plan, execute it TDD-first, and stop only after make check, make lint, and make test pass or the plan must be switched back to TO BE VERIFIED

- read the active task markdown and its referenced plan at .ralph/tasks/story-19-source-sql-emitter-image/02-task-emit-the-required-cockroach-changefeed-sql-from-the-one-time-setup-image_plans/2026-04-19-cockroach-changefeed-sql-plan.md
- confirmed the task is in NOW EXECUTE mode; the approved work is to add explicit cursor changefeed SQL emission, clean up the setup-sql boundary, and keep runner detached from source-side setup
- read the improve-code-boundaries directory listing to confirm the intended smell focus is mixed config ownership and render/planning coupling
- should do next: inspect the current implementation and tests, then start the first red slice by adding or tightening one failing behavior test before any product edits

- wrote the first red slice in crates/setup-sql/tests/bootstrap_contract.rs so emit-cockroach-sql now had to expose a named cluster_logical_timestamp step and a cursor placeholder in each CREATE CHANGEFEED statement
- implemented the green path: setup-sql now renders a per-database typed Cockroach setup plan with explicit cursor handoff comments, split mixed config ownership into config/cockroach.rs + config/postgres_grants.rs + config/table_name.rs, and switched runner test harnesses off the setup-sql Rust crate and onto the public setup-sql binary path
- targeted verification: cargo test -p setup-sql emit_cockroach_sql_defaults_to_text_and_supports_simple_json_output -- --exact passes after the refactor
- should do next: compile runner test support and then run broader required lanes, fixing the next failures one at a time until make check and make test are green
